news.title: HC allows Bengaluru development body to invite applications for Karanth layout but bars site allotments
news.para: The Karnataka High Court modified its earlier order regarding the Dr K Shivaram Karanth Layout.  The Bangalore Development Authority can now invite applications for sites.  However, allotments require prior court permission, which is a key restriction.  This modification addresses the authority's mounting interest burden on project loans.  The court considered the BDA's financial position and loan pressures.

news.title: ITC Hotels net profit up 36% at ₹181.91 crore; to acquire GHK Hospitality & Infrastructures
news.para: ITC Hotels reported a thirty-six percent profit increase for the first quarter.  Consolidated revenue from operations also saw a rise compared to the previous year.  The company announced the acquisition of GHK Hospitality & Infrastructures Ltd for one hundred fifty-five crore.  This acquisition will expand ITC Hotels' owned-asset portfolio in Ahmedabad.  The hotel business revenue grew while the new residences vertical also contributed.

news.title: Odisha government has begun appointing a new member to RERA
news.para: The Odisha government has begun appointing a new member to the Real Estate Regulatory Authority. This appointment follows the earlier departure of the previous administration member. The chairperson's post at the Odisha Real Estate Appellate Tribunal also remains vacant. Stakeholders express concerns about delays affecting dispute resolution processes. Timely appointments are crucial for consumer protection and sector confidence.

news.title: Over 10,600 Ahmedabad civic body housing units found unsafe, redevelopment begins
news.para: Ahmedabad Municipal Corporation is redeveloping over 10,600 unsafe housing units. This initiative follows a structural survey of aging civic body-owned residential buildings. Redevelopment has begun for nearly 8,000 units, with more awaiting resident consent. The South Zone has the highest concentration of dangerous AMC housing. This urgent drive addresses recent monsoon-related building collapses across the city.

news.title: Pune builder, partner booked for duping retiree of ₹12.58 crore over a decade in Pune
news.para: Pune police registered a cheating case against a builder and his partner.  They allegedly defrauded an 80-year-old man of Rs 12.58 crore over ten years.  The builder failed to deliver six flats after taking Rs 11 crore investment.  Later, they promised three shops but never delivered possession of those.  The elderly man approached police after finding no construction work had started.

news.title: NCDRC directs Kakade Construction to give two flats or refund money with interest
news.para: The NCDRC ruled that delayed project delivery constitutes deficiency in service and unfair trade practice. A builder must refund homebuyers with interest and compensation for inordinate delays. Kakade Construction Company has three months to deliver two flats or refund Rs3.01 lakh. The refund includes 15% annual interest as per the agreement's clause. The construction firm failed to file a written response to the allegations.

news.title: Ranchi I-T surveys detect ₹50 crore undisclosed income at realty groups
news.para: The Income Tax investigation wing has detected undisclosed income worth Rs 50 crore.  Surveys were conducted on five premises of Panache Realtors and Dayanand Modi Group.  I-T teams are analyzing documents and digital data, including chats related to cash transactions.  Partners of both real estate groups are currently being questioned by authorities.  Tax evasion was allegedly found in Rosewood apartment projects in Lalpur.

news.title: WeWork India’s net loss narrows to ₹4.61 crore in Q1 FY27
news.para: WeWork India's ’s total income increased 28.4 per cent to ₹700.74 crore during the quarter. Its operational portfolio expanded to 79 centres with 9.1 million sq. ft. of space and 1.34 lakh desks.

news.title: Ajmera Realty sales value rises 35% to ₹146 crore in Q1 FY27
news.para: Ajmera Realty recorded sales value of ₹146 crore in Q1 FY27, up 35% year-on-year, while collections stood at ₹173 crore. Carpet area sold declined 31% year-on-year to 43,737 sq ft.

news.title: Prestige Estates records ₹6,579 crore pre-sales in Q1 FY27
news.para: Prestige Estates recorded pre-sales of ₹6,579 crore in Q1 FY27, with sales volume of 6.04 million sq ft and 3,337 units sold. Collections stood at ₹4,802 crore during the quarter.

news.title: Delhi development body's revenue jumps 120% in Q1 FY27
news.para: Delhi Development Authority housing sales reached Rs 1,020 crores in the first quarter. This revenue marks a significant increase from the previous financial year's sales. Narela emerged as the top contributor, selling 1,153 flats during this period. Infrastructure development and improved connectivity are supporting this housing growth. Ongoing schemes are available online on a first-come, first-served basis.

news.title: Imperial Holding Group’s long-term vision for luxury real estate in India
news.para: India's luxury real estate market sees a shift towards wellness and lifestyle. Affluent buyers now prioritize privacy and quality of life in their homes. Imperial Holding Group is developing premium mountain communities like Auramah Valley. These developments focus on wellness, nature, and community for residents. The company aims to build destinations offering sustainable value and long-term appreciation.

news.title: Andhra Pradesh government plans to issue property cards for assets in urban areas
news.para: Andhra Pradesh plans to introduce property cards for all urban properties. This reform aims to create a single, authenticated record of ownership. The initiative will integrate revenue, registration, and municipal records into one digital document. Detailed surveys using modern technologies will prepare accurate digital records for issuance. This system will simplify transactions and prevent fraudulent ownership claims.

news.title: HC asks Uttar Pradesh government to consider grievance forum for apartment owner-RWA disputes
news.para: The Allahabad High Court has directed the Uttar Pradesh government to consider establishing a dedicated forum. This forum will address disputes between apartment owners and their Residents Welfare Associations. The court noted a lack of effective mechanisms for resolving such internal association conflicts. It dismissed a petition challenging RWA decisions on gate closures and parking charges. The ruling highlights the need for a specialized body to handle these common issues.

news.title: Aurum PropTech to acquire Locon Solutions for ₹458 crore
news.para: Aurum PropTech’s board has approved the acquisition of 100% stake in Locon Solutions, owner of Housing(dot)com, from REA India. The transaction will be carried out through a share swap involving preferential allotment of 1.98 crore equity shares.

news.title: Delhi government probing into building plans cleared without IFC payment
news.para: Delhi government is investigating building plans approved without infrastructure charges.  Thousands of crores in revenue loss are estimated due to this evasion.  Many property owners allegedly used fake documents for approvals.  The government will recover dues and may seal properties if payment is not made.  This action follows a reduction in infrastructure charges by up to seventy percent.

news.title: Real estate capital inflows rise 32% to $8.5 billion in H1 2026: Report
news.para: India’s real estate sector recorded equity capital inflows of $8.5 billion in H1 2026, up 32% year-on-year, according to CBRE. Domestic investors accounted for about 92% of total inflows in Q2 2026.

news.title: Chennai civic body extends special property tax collection camps till July 31
news.para: Chennai Corporation extends property tax collection camps until July 31.  Nearly half of property owners have not yet paid their dues.  The civic body aims to recover pending property tax through intensive collection efforts.  Property tax is a vital revenue source for essential civic works.  Owners are urged to pay by month-end to avoid penal interest.

news.title: Housing project stalled for 12 years, consumer panel orders YEIDA to refund flat buyer
news.para: A consumer commission ordered Yamuna Expressway Industrial Development Authority to refund Rs 2.8 lakh.  The authority failed to deliver a flat under its 2014 housing scheme.  Buyer Omveer Singh sought a refund after the scheme did not materialize.  YEIDA argued Singh defaulted on payments, but the commission disagreed.  The authority must now pay the registration fee with interest and litigation costs.

news.title: Jaypee deal to help Dalmia Bharat have pan-India presence: MD
news.para: Dalmia Bharat plans significant pan-India expansion, primarily through organic growth. The company is also open to strategic acquisitions to accelerate market entry. Recent acquisitions have boosted capacity, with further scaling planned by FY28 and FY31. Greenfield projects and capacity enhancements are underway, including a major plant in Kadapa. Dalmia Bharat aims to strengthen its position in key demand hotspots across India.
